prompt: I want to create a new microservice for managing products in my e-commerce platform. The service should be called 'inventory', the entity is a 'Product' with fields: name (string), sku (string, unique), price (number), quantity (number), category (string), and status (active/inactive/discontinued). Operations needed: create product, get product by id, deactivate product (sets status to inactive), and delete product. Use the same hexagonal architecture as the orders service — Express + NestJS, Postgres for writes, MongoDB for reads, Redis cache, Kafka events, and OpenTelemetry tracing. Please scaffold the full service structure with all files.

assertions.name: domain-no-imports
assertions.description: The domain/product.ts file has no framework or library imports

assertions.name: ports-are-abstract-classes
assertions.description: All port definitions use 'abstract class' keyword, not 'interface'

assertions.name: use-cases-wrap-telemetry-span
assertions.description: Every use case's execute() method wraps its body in this.telemetry.span(...)

assertions.name: redis-cache-ttl
assertions.description: The Redis cache adapter's set() call includes a TTL (EX parameter)

assertions.name: status-as-const
assertions.description: Product status enum is defined as 'as const' object, not TypeScript enum

assertions.name: graceful-shutdown-allsettled
assertions.description: Express bootstrap uses Promise.allSettled for graceful shutdown
